notes:
## v0.3.0

First tagged release. Recent changes:

- DRAGON-176: auto-draft releases with commit notes; publishing ships the update
- DRAGON-175: silence Linux dead-code warnings on the macOS-only update symbols
- DRAGON-175: in-app updates from the About page, fed from GitHub Pages
- DRAGON-174: fix Linux clippy doc-list warning in tray.rs
- DRAGON-174: unified session-icon presence + replace systray/toolbar option
- DRAGON-173: restructure the in-recording menu (uniform, with a Capture Menu submenu)
- DRAGON-173: silence Linux dead-code warnings (quit_enabled, resident_menu_labels)
- DRAGON-173: Linux resident mode (always-on ksni tray, full mac parity)
- DRAGON-172: fix Linux clippy doc-list warning in tray.rs
- DRAGON-172: recording-controls parity (mac toolbar+daemon, Linux tray menu+icon)
- DRAGON-170: fix Linux zero-warnings build (TrayEvent::Cancel dead_code)
- DRAGON-171: bump version to 0.1.0 (DRAGON-160..171 release)
- DRAGON-170: paused icon state, Cancel recording menu item, Quit disabled while recording
- DRAGON-170: widen the status icon's edge gaps (shorter corner stubs)
- DRAGON-170: status icon shows corner brackets instead of a dashed border
- DRAGON-170: dashed status icon border in both states
- DRAGON-171: macOS preview-editor media ducking via MediaRemote proxy
- DRAGON-170: daemon in-recording actions + two-state menu-bar icon
- DRAGON-169: default max bitrate 8000 kbps
- DRAGON-168: macOS recording delivers frames at the encode size (SCK GPU scaling)
- DRAGON-167: fix the Linux PipeWire stale-frame shape + Linux anti-freeze test nets
- DRAGON-164: fix macOS recorded video frozen frame (stale-feed under backpressure)
- DRAGON-161: fix Linux zero-warnings build + test (deferred Linux verification pass)
- DRAGON-163: encoder benchmark tests true monitor dimensions with a monitor picker
- DRAGON-162: fix 5K monitor recording on both encoders (macOS)
- DRAGON-161: make the platform seams explicit (seam map + recording-worker contract)
- DRAGON-161: extract the shared media-clock stop tail into a portable, tested core
- DRAGON-160: bound the macOS SCK stop-tail video write (preview no longer spins)
